evals.prompt: I created a PostgreSQL schema with ENUM types for status fields, ARRAY columns for tags, and UUID primary keys. Now NocoDB can't properly read some of these. What did I do wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies all three issues: ENUM→text, ARRAY→json, UUID→serial/bigserial. Provides ALTER TABLE migration SQL for each fix. References the verification checklist.

expectations.text: Identifies ENUM as incompatible and recommends replacing with text
Identifies ARRAY as incompatible and recommends replacing with json
Identifies UUID PK as incompatible and recommends serial/bigserial
Provides ALTER TABLE SQL to fix at least one of the issues
References or presents the verification checklist

evals.prompt: Before I connect my PostgreSQL database to NocoBase as an external source, what should I check? Give me a validation checklist.
evals.expected_output: The 10-item verification checklist covering: serial PK, FK type matching, ON DELETE NO ACTION, indexes on FKs, UNIQUE for 1:1, composite PK for M2M, no ARRAY/ENUM/POINT/INHERITS, json not jsonb, text for selects, snake_case naming.

expectations.text: Check that PKs use serial or bigserial
Check that FK column types match parent PK types
Check FK constraints use ON DELETE NO ACTION
Check indexes exist on all FK columns
Check for absence of ARRAY, ENUM, POINT, POLYGON, INHERITS
Check json is used instead of jsonb
Check snake_case naming, no reserved words
